Functions for configuring and displaying dialog windows, including text, icons, and animations.

# Purpose
The code defines a set of functions for managing and displaying a `Dialog` structure in a user interface. It provides functionality to configure various aspects of a dialog, such as its fullscreen state, text content, text and background colors, icon, and animation direction. The code also allows setting a vibration effect when the dialog appears, a timeout for the dialog's visibility, and callbacks for handling dialog events. The functions interact with other components like `window`, `text_layer`, and `kino_layer` to achieve these configurations.

The code is part of a C library intended to be used in applications that require dialog management. It includes functions to allocate and manage memory for dialog text, set the dialog's appearance, and control its behavior. The code uses external libraries, as indicated by the inclusion of headers like `applib/ui/window.h` and `applib/applib_malloc.auto.h`, suggesting that it is part of a larger application framework. The functions defined in this file do not provide a public API directly but are likely intended to be used internally within the application to manage dialog components.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `dialog.h`
- `applib/ui/window.h`
- `applib/applib_malloc.auto.h`
- `string.h`

### dialog\_set\_fullscreen<!-- {{#callable:dialog_set_fullscreen}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/dialog.c#L24>)

Sets the fullscreen state of a dialog's window.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``dialog``: A pointer to a `Dialog` structure that contains the window to modify.
    - ``is_fullscreen``: A boolean value that indicates whether to set the window to fullscreen (`true`) or not (`false`).
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`window_set_fullscreen`](<../window.c.md#window_set_fullscreen>) function with the `dialog->window` and `is_fullscreen` as arguments.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_set_fullscreen`](<../window.c.md#window_set_fullscreen>)

[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/dialog.c#L28>)

    - ``show_status_layer``: A boolean value that determines whether to show (`true`) or hide (`false`) the status bar layer.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assigns the value of `show_status_layer` to the `show_status_layer` member of the `dialog` structure.
- **Output**: No return value.

### dialog\_set\_text<!-- {{#callable:dialog_set_text}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/dialog.c#L32>)

Sets the text of a dialog by allocating memory for the text buffer and copying the text into it.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``dialog``: A pointer to a `Dialog` structure where the text will be set.
    - ``text``: A constant character pointer to the text string to be set in the dialog.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`dialog_set_text_buffer`](<#dialog_set_text_buffer>) with `NULL` and `false` to clear any existing text buffer.
    - Calculates the length of the input text using `strlen`.
    - Sets `dialog->is_buffer_owned` to `true` to indicate ownership of the buffer.
    - Allocates memory for the text buffer using `applib_malloc` with a size of `len + 1` to accommodate the null terminator.
    - Copies the input text into the allocated buffer using `strncpy`.
    - Sets the text of the dialog's text layer using [`text_layer_set_text`](<../text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_text>).
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `dialog` structure in place.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`dialog_set_text_buffer`](<#dialog_set_text_buffer>)
    - [`text_layer_set_text`](<../text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_text>)


---
### dialog\_set\_text\_buffer<!-- {{#callable:dialog_set_text_buffer}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/dialog.c#L41>)

Sets the text buffer for a dialog and manages its ownership.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``dialog``: A pointer to a `Dialog` structure that contains the dialog data.
    - ``buffer``: A pointer to a character array that represents the text buffer to set for the dialog.
    - ``take_ownership``: A boolean value indicating whether the dialog should take ownership of the buffer.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if the dialog already has a buffer and if it owns that buffer.
    - If the dialog owns the existing buffer, it frees the buffer using `applib_free`.
    - Sets the `is_buffer_owned` flag of the dialog to the value of `take_ownership`.
    - Assigns the `buffer` to the dialog's `buffer` field.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `dialog` structure in place.

### dialog\_set\_text\_color<!-- {{#callable:dialog_set_text_color}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/dialog.c#L49>)

Sets the text color of a dialog, defaulting to black if color is not supported.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``dialog``: A pointer to a `Dialog` structure whose text color will be set.
    - ``text_color``: A `GColor` value representing the desired text color.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assigns `text_color` to `dialog->text_color` if color is supported, otherwise assigns `GColorBlack`.
    - Calls [`text_layer_set_text_color`](<../text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_text_color>) to apply the text color to the dialog's text layer.
- **Output**: No return value; modifies the `dialog` structure in place.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`text_layer_set_text_color`](<../text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_text_color>)


---
### dialog\_set\_background\_color<!-- {{#callable:dialog_set_background_color}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/dialog.c#L54>)

Sets the background color of a dialog window, defaulting to white if color is not supported.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``dialog``: A pointer to a `Dialog` structure whose window's background color will be set.
    - ``background_color``: A `GColor` value representing the desired background color for the dialog window.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`window_set_background_color`](<../window.c.md#window_set_background_color>) with the dialog's window and a color determined by `PBL_IF_COLOR_ELSE` macro.
    - Uses `background_color` if color is supported, otherwise defaults to `GColorWhite`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the background color of the dialog's window.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_set_background_color`](<../window.c.md#window_set_background_color>)

### dialog\_set\_icon<!-- {{#callable:dialog_set_icon}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/dialog.c#L58>)

Sets the icon for a dialog and manages its animation state.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``dialog``: A pointer to a `Dialog` structure where the icon will be set.
    - ``icon_id``: An unsigned 32-bit integer representing the ID of the icon to set.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if the current icon ID of the dialog is the same as the new `icon_id`.
    - If the icon IDs are the same, rewind and play the animation of the icon layer to preserve behavior, then return.
    - If the icon IDs are different, update the dialog's icon ID to the new `icon_id`.
    - Check if the dialog's window is loaded.
    - If the window is loaded, set the reel of the icon layer with the new icon resource.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `Dialog` structure in place.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_is_loaded`](<../window.c.md#window_is_loaded>)


---

- **Output**: No output is returned.

### dialog\_set\_callbacks<!-- {{#callable:dialog_set_callbacks}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/dialog.c#L85>)

Sets the callback functions and context for a `Dialog` object.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``dialog``: A pointer to a `Dialog` object where the callbacks will be set.
    - ``callbacks``: A pointer to a `DialogCallbacks` structure containing the callback functions to be set.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to a context that will be passed to the callback functions.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `callbacks` is `NULL`.
    - If `callbacks` is `NULL`, set `dialog->callbacks` to an empty `DialogCallbacks` structure and return.
    - If `callbacks` is not `NULL`, copy the `callbacks` structure to `dialog->callbacks`.
    - Set `dialog->callback_context` to `callback_context`.
- **Output**: No return value; modifies the `dialog` object in place.

### dialog\_appear<!-- {{#callable:dialog_appear}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/dialog.c#L100>)

Activates the icon animation for a dialog if an icon reel is present.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``dialog``: A pointer to a `Dialog` structure that contains the icon layer to be animated.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Get the `icon_layer` from the `dialog`.
    - Check if the `icon_layer` has a reel using `kino_layer_get_reel`.
    - If a reel is present, call `kino_layer_play` to start the animation.
- **Output**: No return value.
